channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Nairobi Government Launches 48-Hour Plan to Address Flood Damage
item.description: Nairobi city government, in partnership with the national government, has launched an urgent 48-hour action plan to tackle the ongoing flooding affecting various parts of the capital. Governor Johnson...

item.title: ACM co-owner Alex Waislitz addresses serious allegations against Antony Catalano
item.description: Alex Waislitz, co-owner of Australian Community Media (ACM) and partner in View Media Group, has publicly addressed serious allegations against his business partner, Antony Catalano. Catalano, 59, has...

item.title: Philippines Reaffirms Sovereignty Over Scarborough Shoal Amid China Dispute
item.description: The Department of Foreign Affairs (DFA) has strongly reiterated that Scarborough Shoal, also known as Bajo de Masinloc or Panatag Shoal, is part of Philippine territory. This statement came after Chin...

item.title: Victorian Teachers Reject Government Pay Offer, Statewide Strike Planned
item.description: Victoria faces its first statewide teacher strike since Labor returned to government, following the Australian Education Union's rejection of a government pay offer. The Allan government proposed a 17...

item.title: Federal Government to Release March Salaries and Pensions Ahead of Eid
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has instructed all federal government departments to disburse March salaries and pensions ahead of Eidul Fitr, so that public servants can prepare for the festival withou...

item.title: Ex-Chief of Staff Cashes Out $50,000 in Vacation Pay During Federal Corruption Investigation
item.description: Dana Williamson, former chief of staff for California Governor Gavin Newsom, left her position amid a federal corruption investigation and received over $50,000 in unused vacation pay. Payroll records...

item.title: Hollywood Filmmakers Engage Politically on Screen but Remain Cautious in Speeches
item.description: The 2026 Academy Awards highlighted a curious tension in Hollywood between cinematic politics and public commentary. Many winning films tackled pressing contemporary issues or historical injustices, y...

item.title: Paul Thomas Anderson’s 'One Battle After Another' sweeps Oscars with six wins, including Best Picture and Best Director
item.description: The darkly comic thriller 'One Battle After Another' emerged as the big winner at the 2026 Oscars, taking home six trophies including Best Picture, Best Director and Best Adapted Screenplay for Paul T...

item.title: Israel begins limited ground operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon
item.description: The Israeli military announced on March 16, 2026, that it had initiated limited ground operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon. According to the Israel Defense Forces (IDF), these targeted in...

item.title: Bangladesh Faces Power Shortages Amid Global Energy Crisis
item.description: Bangladesh is grappling with significant electricity shortages due to a global energy crunch triggered by the war in the Persian Gulf. The government has taken drastic measures, including closing univ...

item.title: Rulani Mokwena detained in Algeria over alleged currency violation
item.description: South African football coach Rulani Mokwena has been detained in Algeria after reportedly breaching the country’s strict foreign currency regulations. The 39-year-old, who recently resigned as head co...

item.title: Kenyan Citizens No Longer Eligible for Russian Military Following Diplomatic Talks
item.description: Kenyans will no longer be allowed to join the Russian military after talks between Musalia Mudavadi and Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov. The agreement comes after concerns surfaced that some Ke...

item.title: Winnipeg Jets and Cole Perfetti Bounce Back with Strong Performances
item.description: The Winnipeg Jets managed to turn around what threatened to be a disappointing homestand with key victories over Colorado and St. Louis. After consecutive losses to Anaheim and the Rangers, the team f...

item.title: Pakistan Records $479 Million Surplus in February Current Account
item.description: Pakistan’s current account has seen a welcome turnaround, posting a surplus of $479 million in February, according to the latest figures from the State Bank of Pakistan (SBP). This is a significant sh...

item.title: House Votes to Eliminate Travel Tax for Filipino Travelers
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The House of Representatives approved a bill on March 16, 2026, seeking to abolish the travel tax imposed on Filipino travelers for international flights. House Bill No. 8464, wh...

item.title: Delhi Assembly Speaker Supports Om Birla Amid Opposition Motion
item.description: Delhi Assembly Speaker Vijender Gupta expressed strong disapproval of the recent opposition move in the Lok Sabha aimed at removing Speaker Om Birla. In a letter addressed to Birla, Gupta emphasised t...

item.title: Kerala Man Killed by Neighbour in Suspected Revenge Case
item.description: In a tragic incident at Manjapra near Angamaly, Kerala, a 50-year-old man named Jose Chakku, also known as Kilukkan Jose, was allegedly shot dead by his neighbour, Pauly Jose, on the evening of March ...

item.title: Court rules parliamentary staff dismissals over certificates were unfair, orders Sh12 million payout
item.description: Six parliamentary employees who were dismissed over allegations of presenting forged academic certificates have won a total of Sh12 million in damages after the Employment and Labour Relations Court f...

item.title: The Brontë Sisters' Novels Ranked from Worst to Best
item.description: This Guardian article ranks the seven published novels by the Brontë sisters — Charlotte, Emily, and Anne — from least to most accomplished, prompted by renewed interest due to Emerald Fennell’s recen...

item.title: Helicopter Crash In Tema Community 1 Kills Two, Authorities Investigating
item.description: Two people don die after one helicopter crash for near Oninku Park, Tema Community 1, Ghana. Security people and aviation authorities rush go the scene sharp sharp, cordon off the area and start preli...
